name: ExtensionNutritionIntake_ConsumedItem
title: R5: What food or fluid product or item was consumed (new)
purpose:
This extension is part of the cross-version definitions generated to enable use of the
element `NutritionIntake.consumedItem` as defined in FHIR R5
in FHIR R4.

The source element is defined as:
`NutritionIntake.consumedItem` 1..* `BackboneElement`

Across FHIR versions, the element set has been mapped as:
* R5 `NutritionIntake.consumedItem` 1..* `BackboneElement`

Following are the generation technical comments:
Element `NutritionIntake.consumedItem` is not mapped to FHIR R4, since FHIR R5 `NutritionIntake` is not mapped.
Element `NutritionIntake.consumedItem` has no mapping targets in FHIR R4. Typically, this is because the element has been added (is a new element).

element.short: The type of food or fluid product
element.definition: Indicates what a category of item that was consumed: e.g., food, fluid, enteral, etc.

element.short: R5: Code that identifies the food or fluid product that was consumed (new)
element.comment:
Element `NutritionIntake.consumedItem.nutritionProduct` is part of an existing definition because parent element `NutritionIntake.consumedItem` requires a cross-version extension.
Element `NutritionIntake.consumedItem.nutritionProduct` is not mapped to FHIR R4, since FHIR R5 `NutritionIntake` is not mapped.
Element `NutritionIntake.consumedItem.nutritionProduct` has no mapping targets in FHIR R4. Typically, this is because the element has been added (is a new element).
If only a code is specified, then it needs to be a code for a specific product. If more information is required, then the use of a new nutritionProduct resource is recommended.

element.short: Flag to indicate if the food or fluid item was refused or otherwise not consumed
element.definition: Indicator when a patient is in a setting where it is helpful to know if food was not consumed, such as it was refused, held (as in tube feedings), or otherwise not provided. If a consumption is being recorded from an app, such as MyFitnessPal, this indicator will likely not be used.
